assignments.direction: Implement a branch and bound algorithm with depth-first search and lower bounds based on machine workloads and job remaining times.
Explore dispatching heuristics (e.g., Shortest Processing Time, Most Work Remaining) and evaluate their makespan performance on standard benchmarks.
Develop a simulated annealing approach with a geometric cooling schedule and neighborhood moves that swap operations on critical paths.
Design a genetic algorithm using a permutation-based representation for operations, with order crossover and shift mutation operators.
Investigate tabu search with a short-term memory tabu list and neighborhood structures focused on inserting operations into new positions.
Apply ant colony optimization, defining pheromone trails on operation sequences and updating based on makespan of constructed schedules.
Explore a reinforcement learning agent using Q-learning, with states representing job completion status and actions selecting next operations.
Create a hybrid method that initializes schedules with dispatching rules and refines them using iterative local search with swap moves.
